title: Midjourney V8.1 is here, and it feels like coming home
text:
Midjourney V8.1 is here, and it feels like coming home
After the V8.0 stumble, Midjourney delivers a version that’s fast, flexible, and back to what it does best.
Geeky Animals
Apr 15, 2026
∙ Paid
4
1
Share
Give a gift subscription
Midjourney dropped V8.1 today, and I’ve been testing it for several hours.
Long story short, Midjourney is back on track.
If V8.0 left you disappointed, you’re not alone. Midjourney developers themselves seem to feel the same way. They plan to decommission V8.0 just a few weeks after V8.1’s release. That word, “decommission,” is unusual. Normally, older versions just stay available. This time, they’re pulling the plug entirely, which tells you something about how they view the V8.0 experiment.
But let’s move on to V8.1, because it deserves the attention.
I agree with Midjourney’s own description: V8.1 offers a “consistent and familiar aesthetic in the spirit of V7.” That’s exactly how it feels. The aesthetics are back. The quality is there. And it’s faster.
If you’re short on time, here are my top 3 takeaways
Fast mode is really fast.
I’m quite impressed with the speed improvement across the board.
Personalization codes are more flexible.
V8.1 works with older profile codes without fuss. V8.0 would reject codes that weren’t the newest versions. That’s no longer the case.
Text rendering looks better.
I’ve compared several outputs against V7, and V8.1 handles typography more accurately.
Now let me walk you through the details.
Speed and cost improvements
Midjourney made V8.1 cheaper and faster across both resolution modes.
Standard resolution (SD) is 50% faster and 25% cheaper. At full quality, SD in V8.1 runs as fast as V7’s draft mode, which makes it great for exploring and iterating quickly. If you don’t need the extra resolution and want to save on costs, SD is a solid option.
HD mode is 3x faster and 3x cheaper. It’s so much cheaper that Midjourney made HD the default for V8.1. Keep in mind that HD files can be quite large if you need to share them, so SD is still there when you need lighter files.
A warning about “Run as HD”
Midjourney added a button called “Run as HD” that might give you the wrong impression. It sounds like you can generate in SD first, pick your favorite image, and then upscale it to HD.
That’s not how it works.
When you hit “Run as HD,” Midjourney reruns your entire prompt as an HD job based on the job’s “Seed”. And seed can be unreliable and you may not get the same image back. It’s not an upscaler. It just runs your job again at HD resolution based on the seed. Upscalers aren’t available yet for V8.
Pro Tip
: If you want HD results, run in HD from the start. Don’t count on “Run as HD” to preserve a specific image you liked from an SD batch.
Run as HD is misleading. It does not let you upscale a specific image.
In the following sections, I cover:
Side-by-side examples across photographic, cinematic, and fashion styles
Sref mining results compared to V8.0
Testing older personalization codes from almost a year ago
Prompts that failed in V8.0 but work in V8.1
Typography accuracy and artistic letterform tests
What’s coming next for V8
